key_insights.insight: Limiting beliefs often serve a purpose, but identifying and questioning that purpose can help dismantle them.
key_insights.quote: How are they serving us right now and how are they not serving us? Because sometimes we're believing them because they're serving us for a purpose.

key_insights.insight: Confidence is built on past experiences, and consciously choosing to recall positive experiences strengthens self-belief.
key_insights.quote: The trick is to consciously recall the positive experience, the past successes.

key_insights.insight: If there aren't direct positive experiences available, you can transfer the attributes from other successes into similar situations to improve confidence.
key_insights.quote: If there's nothing in the bank exactly of that experience, there's chances are there's something similar.

key_insights.insight: It's vital to focus on intangible qualities and strengths rather than only tangible achievements to build robust self-belief.
key_insights.quote: If we go through life only assessing our success on the tangible stuff then our confidence, our self-belief actually, and they're two different things, will be quite fragile.

key_insights.insight: Self-worth should be intrinsic and not depend on external validation from certificates or trophies.
key_insights.quote: My self-worth isn't based on a certificate and a trophy and an award.

key_insights.insight: When faced with a challenge, concentrating on weaknesses makes confidence fragile, while focusing on strengths is crucial.
key_insights.quote: If I'm sat on the start line of the world championships and I'm focused on my weaknesses and all the things I need to avoid, my confidence is fragile.

actionable_advice.advice: Conduct a 'strengths audit' to identify your transferable skills and attributes.
actionable_advice.how_to: List your past successes in different areas, analyze the attributes that enabled them, and practice consciously applying those to new situations.

actionable_advice.advice: Challenge limiting beliefs by assessing their pros and cons.
actionable_advice.how_to: Specifically write down the benefits you derive from holding a limiting belief and compare it to the ways that belief hinders you to determine if it still serves you.

actionable_advice.advice: Build a bank of positive experiences to counteract negative thought patterns.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively recall and visualize past successes to reinforce feelings of competence when facing new challenges.

memorable_stories: The speaker's personal story of overcoming poor grades and societal expectations through self-belief and skills identification.
contrarian_claims: That focusing solely on tangible achievements can lead to a fragile sense of self-worth, and intrinsic attributes are more valuable.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 28 - How To Overcome Your Limiting Beliefs: Anna Hemmings
